title: Draft Farm Bill Attacks Foundational Protections from Pesticides for Farmers, Consumers, and Environment - Beyond Pesticides Daily News Blog
description: (Beyond Pesticides, February 26, 2026)¬†The chair of the Agriculture Committee in the U.S. House of Representatives, in releasing the Republican 2026 Farm Bill¬†draft last Friday afternoon, is challenging three core safeguards that are seen as critical to the health of farmers, consumers and the environment‚Äîjudicial review of chemical manufacturers‚Äô failure to warn about pesticide hazards, the democratic right of local governments in coordination with states to protect residents from pesticide use, and local site-specific action to ensure the safety of air, water, and land from pesticides. The draft Farm bill language in three separate sections: (i) prohibits lawsuits by farmers and consumers harmed by pesticides for which manufacturers failed to provide complete safety warnings (Section 10205); (ii) takes away the authority of local governments to protect residents and the local environment from pesticide use (Section 10206), and; (iii) repeals requirements in numerous federal statutes to protect against local pesticide contamination that could affect waterways, drinking water, federal projects, endangered species, migratory birds, and toxic waste¬†(Section 10207). Beyond Pesticides responded with a nationwide action to Tell members of the U.S. House of Representatives to stop provisions in the Farm Bill that shield chemical companies from liability for the harm caused by […]

name: EPA Rollbacks
title:
The Trump EPA just announced that it is repealing the 2009 endangerment finding, which is the scientific determination that climate pollution endangers public health and welfare.

This is not a… | Manish Bapna | 23 comments
description:
The Trump EPA just announced that it is repealing the 2009 endangerment finding, which is the scientific determination that climate pollution endangers public health and welfare.

This is not a technical change. It is core to nearly all federal climate protections for the past 15 years.

If this repeal is allowed to stand, it would be the single biggest attack in U.S. history on the federal government’s ability to protect people from climate pollution.

And make no mistake about what this means in real life: more dangerous heat, more wildfires, more extreme storms, more air pollution, and more health harms, with American families left to absorb the cost.

This move is part of a growing pattern of hostility toward the American public: stripping protections and making people less safe, all while billionaires profit.
NRDC will not let this stand. We will see them in court — and we will win. | 23 comments on LinkedIn
